generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Resistive heating element embedded within the Styrofoam enclosure that provides precise thermal input to maintain setpoint temperatures in the incubator system."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Air circulation module with 0.02 m³/s airflow rate (~42 CFM) controlled via RabbitMQ message queuing system for uniform temperature distribution within the incubator enclosure."
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer with ±0.5°C accuracy and 10-bit resolution monitoring internal thermal zone 1 of the incubator."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer with ±0.5°C accuracy and 10-bit resolution monitoring internal thermal zone 2 of the incubator."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer with ±0.5°C accuracy and 10-bit resolution monitoring external environmental conditions affecting the incubator."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance InternalTemperatureData1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
]

instance InternalTemperatureData2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PhysicsBasedThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "High-fidelity physics-based analytical model derived from differential equations representing dynamic thermal and control system interactions including temperature response and energy consumption."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm, StateEstimator
]

instance SimplifiedBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Optimized simplified behavioral model using empirical relationships and reduced-order approximations for real-time performance under varying operating conditions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StateEstimator
]

instance RawTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw time-series temperature measurements collected at 1Hz from all DS18S20 sensors via 1-Wire protocol before preprocessing."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureData1, InternalTemperatureData2, ExternalTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm
]

instance CalibratedModelParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed model parameters derived from non-linear least squares solver applied to raw sensor data to minimize residual error between simulated and observed behavior."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RawTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PhysicsBasedThermalModel, SimplifiedBehavioralModel
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RabbitMQServer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ message broker enabling real-time data streaming, command/state sharing, and event-driven updates between Raspberry Pi and Digital Twin components."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAcquisitionService, ControlCommandService, MonitoringService, OptimizationService
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi 4 Model B platform implementing bang-bang controller, sensor interfacing, and local data processing for real-time control logic."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAcquisitionService, ControlCommandService
]

instance PythonScipyOptimizer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python Scipy package implementation providing optimization functions for refining controller parameters (hysteresis, setpoint adjustments) and minimizing energy consumption."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService
]

instance CalibrationAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Dynamic calibration algorithms updating model parameters using real-time sensor data to ensure fidelity between Digital Twin and Physical Twin representations."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
]

instance StateEstimator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "State estimation algorithms (implicitly Kalman filter-based) reconciling simulated and observed behavior to refine model accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataAcquisitionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time data acquisition service collecting temperature, fan speed, and heatbed power data from Physical Twin via Raspberry Pi interface."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureDataInsight, DataQualityAssuranceAction
]

instance ControlCommandService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Command execution service translating Digital Twin control decisions into physical actuation commands for fan and heatbed components."
    DTDFVocab:provides ActuationCommandAction
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring service validating system performance against safety and optimality criteria using calibrated models and state estimation."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemHealthInsight, FaultDetectionInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Energy consumption and performance optimization service refining controller parameters to minimize overshoot and improve steady-state accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationInsight, ParameterAdjustmentAction
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive modeling service forecasting system performance under current or hypothetical operating conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceForecastInsight, WhatIfAnalysisInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TemperatureDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Raw temperature measurements from all monitoring points with timestamp information for further processing."
]

instance DataQualityAssuranceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated data validation and preprocessing action ensuring sensor data integrity before model calibration."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ActuationCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Physical actuation command execution for fan and heatbed components based on Digital Twin control decisions."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SystemHealthInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous assessment of Physical Twin health status against predefined safety and operational thresholds."
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time fault detection identifying anomalies in Physical Twin behavior using model-based monitoring algorithms."
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Data-driven recommendations for improving system performance including energy efficiency and temperature regulation."
]

instance ParameterAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of controller parameters (hysteresis, setpoints) based on optimization analysis."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PerformanceForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive forecast of system performance under current operational conditions including energy consumption and thermal stability."
]

instance WhatIfAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulation results evaluating impact of parameter changes (fan speed, heatbed power) on thermal stability and system performance."
]

// C18: Environment
instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains StyrofoamEnclosure, HeatbedActuator, FanActuator, InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance StyrofoamEnclosure : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Primary thermal isolation enclosure made of Styrofoam material containing all internal components and maintaining controlled thermal environment."
    base:isContainedIn IncubatorEnvironment
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PhysicsBasedThermalModel, SimplifiedBehavioralModel, RabbitMQServer, RaspberryPiController, CalibrationAlgorithm, StateEstimator
]

instance IncubatorSystem : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "Thermal incubator system for controlled temperature regulation in biomedical applications."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment IncubatorEnvironment
]

instance DigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:DigitalTwin [
    base:desc "Digital representation of the incubator system for monitoring, simulation, and optimization."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment DigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in this DT system involves the transmission of control commands from the DT to the Physical Twin (PT) via the **RabbitMQ server**, which listens for commands to adjust the PT's components, such as the heatbed and fan. The controller component sends these commands through the RabbitMQ server, which acts as an intermediary for communication. The system lacks explicit details on command formats (e.g., structured messages like temperature setpoints or fan speed adjustments), validation mechanisms (e.g., checksums, digital signatures), or authentication protocols. However, the low-level driver issues the states of the PT based on received commands, and the controller receives measured data from the PT as feedback. Conflict resolution mechanisms are not discussed, nor are there specifics on command execution confirmation or feedback systems beyond the bidirectional data flow implied by the controller's role. Feedback from the PT is implicitly handled through the measured data received by the controller, though no explicit feedback loop or error-handling protocols are detailed.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates with a temporal synchronization framework that integrates real-time data streams from the Physical Twin (PT) via sensor inputs, processed through calibration algorithms to update system models. The document references the use of time intervals in dynamic equations (e.g., differential equations) for behavioral analysis, implying that the DT must align with the operational time scales of the PT. While specific synchronization frequencies (e.g., milliseconds, seconds) or latency windows are not detailed, the system relies on a continuous feedback loop where calibration algorithms adjust models based on incoming data streams. The temporal granularity is implicitly tied to the PT's operational dynamics, requiring low-latency processing to ensure accurate state estimation and predictive simulations. The document mentions 'synchronization' via internal mechanisms (e.g., RabbitMQ for communication) but does not specify exact time-scale constraints or update frequencies beyond the implicit requirement for real-time responsiveness in monitoring and calibration."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows an **integrated architecture pattern** that combines **modelling, simulation, monitoring, and optimization technologies** into a cohesive framework. Key components include:
  - **Data**: Collected from the PT via sensors over time, serving as the foundational input for model calibration and analysis.
  - **Models**: Abstract representations of the PT's cyber and physical aspects, including its environment, parameterized by differential equations or other mathematical formulations.
  - **Algorithms**: Techniques such as **fault detectors**, **supervisory controllers**, **state estimators**, and **optimizers**, which manipulate data and models to generate actionable insights or corrective actions.
  The system employs **resource management** through algorithmic coordination, where outputs from calibration (e.g., updated parameter estimates) inform monitoring systems that assess safety and optimality. **System-wide coordination** is achieved via feedback loops: violations detected by monitors trigger human or algorithmic interventions to adjust the PT's behavior. The document does not specify explicit **orchestration patterns** (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures) or **component integration standards** (e.g., OPC UA, ROS 2)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) as described in the provided documents is centered around the integration of models into the system post-deployment, emphasizing continuous value addition to the Physical Twin (PT). The lifecycle begins with the development of abstract representations (e.g., CAD models or dynamic models) to understand specific system aspects, such as structural or dynamic behavior. Once deployed, these models are not discarded but integrated into the DT framework, which combines **data** (collected via sensors over time), **models** (knowledge of cyber-physical systems and environmental physics), and **algorithms** (e.g., calibration, fault detection, state estimation) to enable real-time monitoring and optimization. The lifecycle governance involves iterative updates: calibration algorithms refine model parameters (e.g., estimating forces acting on a car or environmental dynamics) to ensure accuracy, while monitors assess system performance for safety and optimality. Violations trigger human or algorithmic interventions to adjust the PT, creating a feedback loop. No explicit mention of formal stages like 'Evolution & Retirement' or 'Governance' is provided, but the lifecycle is implicitly iterative and adaptive, relying on continuous data-model-algorithm integration to sustain the PT's operational value."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system prioritizes **model fidelity** and **validity** through structured **validation and verification (V&V) methods**, **uncertainty quantification**, and **quality assurance (QA) processes**. Key aspects include:
- **Model fidelity requirements**: Models must accurately represent **both cyber and physical aspects** of the PT and its environment, including geometric, behavioral, and analytical dimensions. Fidelity is achieved through **calibration algorithms** that adjust model parameters (e.g., differential equation coefficients) using real-time sensor data to minimize discrepancies between virtual and physical behavior.
- **Validation and verification methods**: While not explicitly detailed, the document implies **comparative analysis** between simulated DT outputs and PT observations to validate predictive accuracy. Verification involves **cross-checking model logic** (e.g., differential equations) against known physical principles.
- **Uncertainty sources and quantification**: Uncertainties arise from **sensor noise**, **model simplifications**, and **environmental variability**. The DT framework addresses this through **statistical calibration techniques** and **error bounds** in model parameters, ensuring robustness in predictions.
- **Error detection and correction mechanisms**: **Monitoring algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors) continuously analyze PT data for anomalies or deviations from expected behavior. Corrections are applied via **adaptive model updates** or **human/automated interventions** (e.g., adjusting actuators or reconfiguring control strategies).
- **Quality assurance processes**: The DT retains **original design models** post-deployment to ensure consistency and traceability. QA involves **iterative refinement** of models and algorithms based on operational feedback, with a focus on **safety and optimality** of the PT’s performance.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs **RabbitMQ** as the primary communication protocol for inter-system messaging, facilitating bidirectional data exchange between the Digital Twin and Physical Twin. The network infrastructure includes **Wi-Fi** and **Bluetooth** for connectivity, though no details on network topology (e.g., star, mesh), bandwidth requirements, or latency specifications are provided. Reliability mechanisms are not explicitly described, but the use of RabbitMQ implies fault tolerance through message queuing. Security protocols are not mentioned in the document, and no redundancy mechanisms (e.g., failover, load balancing) are referenced."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is deployed on a **Raspberry Pi** as the primary hosting platform, indicating an on-premise, edge-computing deployment model. Computational and storage requirements are minimal, as the system relies on lightweight Python-based implementations and RabbitMQ for message handling. Scalability mechanisms are not discussed, nor are microservices, containerization, or high-performance computing implied. Availability and reliability are not explicitly addressed, though the simplicity of the setup suggests low complexity in terms of redundancy or failover strategies."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system incorporates **safety-critical mechanisms** to ensure operational integrity of the Physical Twin (PT). Specifically, it employs **calibration algorithms** to update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment with the best parameter estimates, derived from real-time sensor data. These algorithms are integrated into a monitoring framework that evaluates system performance against predefined safety thresholds.

The system includes **fault detection mechanisms** as part of its algorithmic components, which analyze deviations from expected behavior (e.g., via state estimators) to identify anomalies. While the document does not explicitly detail **cybersecurity protocols** or **access control mechanisms**, it references a **safe protocol** in low-level drivers to maintain the device in a stable state, ensuring fail-safe operation. Risk assessment is implicitly addressed through the use of **monitoring algorithms** that trigger corrective actions (e.g., human intervention or automated adjustments) when violations are detected, thereby mitigating operational risks."
]
